name: SOLID PSEUDOPAPILLARY TUMOR OF PANCREAS
definition: SOLID AND PAPILLARY NEOPLASM = SOLID AND CYSTIC TUMOR = PAPILLARY-CYSTIC NEOPLASM = rare slow-growing low-grade malignant tumor; often misclassified as nonfunctioning

demographics.Mean age: 28 (range, 2-85) years; M÷F = 1÷9; predilection for black and East Asian patients
demographics.Path: large well-encapsulated solitary mass with considerable hemorrhagic necrosis + cystic
demographics.Histo: sheets + cords of cells arranged around a fibrovascular stroma
demographics.Location: pancreatic tail (36%), pancreatic head (34%); frequently exophytic obscuring

distribution: pancreatic tail (36%), pancreatic head (34%); frequently exophytic obscuring
discriminating_features: well-encapsulated inhomogeneous round / lobulated pancreatic mass with completely solid ±
may be completely cystic (when complicated by extensive necrosis + internal hemorrhage)
compression of adjacent structures without invasion
fluid-fluid or fluid-debris level (10-20%) ← hematocrit effect
± stippled / punctate / amorphous dystrophic calcification (33%) rimlike in tumor capsule or

general: well-encapsulated inhomogeneous round / lobulated pancreatic mass with completely solid ±

US: echogenic mass with necrotic center / completely cystic with subcapsular rim of tumor
CT: solid tumor component isoattenuating to pancreas
MR: hypointense fibrous capsule on T1WI + T2WI (80-100%)
iso- to hypointense on T1WI compared to pancreas
foci of high SI on T1WI (in 80-100%) + variable T2 signal intensity ← variable
T2 signal intensity close to fluid in mostly cystic variety
enhancing soft-tissue components are uniformly present
early peripheral heterogeneous / ring enhancement greater than pancreas (70%)
gradual accumulation (= progressive fill-in) of contrast material within tumor
hypointense to pancreas on delayed images
ANGIO: hypo- to avascular with displacement of vessels
